taxonomy: Euosma flaviflora is a species of moth in the family Tortricidae. It is a member of the genus Euosma, which is a group of moths primarily found in Southeast Asia. The family Tortricidae is a large group of moths, commonly known as leafrollers, that are known for their distinctive habits of rolling up leaves to form a shelter. The classification of Euosma flaviflora is a part of the larger Lepidoptera order, which includes butterflies and moths. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships between these moths and other related species.
